Analysis of Deforestation

2026-03-31

Data Organization

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.2     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(lme4)      # mixed models
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
library(cluster)   # clustering tools
library(broom)     # tidy model output
library(GGally)    # pair plots
carbon <- read.csv("C:/Users/yllw1/OneDrive/Desktop/Data-399/Project Data + Resources/Data/Country_Carbon_Data.csv")
drivers <- read.csv("C:/Users/yllw1/OneDrive/Desktop/Data-399/Project Data + Resources/Data/Country_Drivers.csv")
primary <- read.csv("C:/Users/yllw1/OneDrive/Desktop/Data-399/Project Data + Resources/Data/Country_Primary_Loss.csv")
tc_loss <- read.csv("C:/Users/yllw1/OneDrive/Desktop/Data-399/Project Data + Resources/Data/Country_Tree_Cover_Loss.csv")


mdd_0108 <- read.csv("C:/Users/yllw1/OneDrive/Desktop/Data-399/Project Data + Resources/Data/Alvarez-Berrios_etal_2021_Data_2001_2008.csv")
mdd_0914 <- read.csv("C:/Users/yllw1/OneDrive/Desktop/Data-399/Project Data + Resources/Data/Alvarez-Berrios_etal_2021_Data_2009_2014.csv")
drivers <- drivers %>%
  select(country, threshold, driver, year, tc_loss_ha)
drivers30 <- drivers %>% filter(threshold == 30)
primary30 <- primary %>% filter(threshold == 30)
tc_loss30  <- tc_loss %>% filter(threshold == 30)
carbon30   <- carbon %>% filter(umd_tree_cover_density_2000__threshold == 30)
# tree cover loss
tc_loss_long <- tc_loss30 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("tc_loss_ha_"),
    names_to = "year",
    names_prefix = "tc_loss_ha_",
    values_to = "tree_cover_loss_ha"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# primary forest loss
primary_long <- primary30 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("tc_loss_ha_"),
    names_to = "year",
    names_prefix = "tc_loss_ha_",
    values_to = "primary_loss_ha"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# carbon emissions
carbon_long <- carbon30 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("gfw_forest_carbon_gross_emissions_") &
      ends_with("__Mg_CO2e"),
    names_to = "year",
    names_pattern = "gfw_forest_carbon_gross_emissions_(\\d+)__Mg_CO2e",
    values_to = "carbon_emissions_MgCO2e"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

# driver data
drivers_wide <- drivers30 %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = driver,
    values_from = tc_loss_ha,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

Merge Data

panel_data <- drivers_wide %>%
  left_join(tc_loss_long %>% select(country, year, tree_cover_loss_ha), 
            by = c("country", "year")) %>%
  left_join(primary_long %>% select(country, year, primary_loss_ha), 
            by = c("country", "year")) %>%
  left_join(carbon_long %>% select(country, year, carbon_emissions_MgCO2e), 
            by = c("country", "year")) %>%
  left_join(
    carbon30 %>%
      select(
        country,
        umd_tree_cover_extent_2000__ha,
        gfw_aboveground_carbon_stocks_2000__Mg_C,
        avg_gfw_aboveground_carbon_stocks_2000__Mg_C_ha.1
      ) %>%
      distinct(),
    by = "country"
  )

madre_all <- bind_rows(mdd_0108, mdd_0914)
head(panel_data)
## # A tibble: 6 × 16
##   country     threshold  year hard_commodities logging other_natural_disturban…¹
##   <chr>           <int> <int>            <int>   <int>                     <int>

## 2 Afghanistan        30  2001                0       3                         2
## 3 Afghanistan        30  2002                0      64                         3
## 4 Afghanistan        30  2003                0      73                         1
## 5 Afghanistan        30  2004                0     143                         1

## #   settlements_infrastructure <int>, wildfire <int>,
## #   shifting_cultivation <int>, tree_cover_loss_ha <int>,
## #   primary_loss_ha <int>, carbon_emissions_MgCO2e <dbl>,
## #   umd_tree_cover_extent_2000__ha <int>,
## #   gfw_aboveground_carbon_stocks_2000__Mg_C <dbl>, …
head(madre_all)

EDA

Trends

panel_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    hard_commodities = sum(hard_commodities, na.rm = TRUE),
    tree_cover_loss_ha = sum(tree_cover_loss_ha, na.rm = TRUE),
    primary_loss_ha = sum(primary_loss_ha, na.rm = TRUE),
    carbon_emissions_MgCO2e = sum(carbon_emissions_MgCO2e, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw()

Scatterplots

ggplot(panel_data, aes(x = hard_commodities, y = carbon_emissions_MgCO2e, color=year)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +
  labs(x='Hard-commodity-driven forest loss (ha)',
       y='Forest carbon emissions (Mg CO2e)',
       title='Hard Commodities vs Cabon Emissions',
       color= 'Year')+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(legend.position = "top")

Pairs plot

eda_vars <- panel_data %>%
  select(
    tree_cover_loss_ha,
    primary_loss_ha,
    carbon_emissions_MgCO2e,
    hard_commodities,
    logging,
    shifting_cultivation,
    wildfire
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

GGally::ggpairs(eda_vars)

Driver Share analysis

driver_share <- drivers30 %>%
  group_by(country, year) %>%
  mutate(total_driver_loss = sum(tc_loss_ha, na.rm = TRUE),
         share = tc_loss_ha / total_driver_loss) %>%
  ungroup()

driver_share %>%
  filter(driver == "Hard commodities") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = share, group = country)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "Hard commodities share of driver-attributed loss")
## Warning: Removed 10 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_summary()`).
## Warning: Removed 10 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_line()`).

Explanation Analysis

MLR

# tree cover loss
lm1 <- lm(
  tree_cover_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation + wildfire + factor(year),
  data = panel_data
)

summary(lm1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tree_cover_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + 
##     shifting_cultivation + wildfire + factor(year), data = panel_data)
## 
## Residuals:

## shifting_cultivation  1.153e+00  4.271e-02  27.006   <2e-16 ***

# primary cover loss
lm2 <- lm(
  primary_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation + wildfire + factor(year),
  data = panel_data
)

summary(lm2)

# carbon emissions

  carbon_emissions_MgCO2e ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation + wildfire + factor(year),
  data = panel_data
)

Mixed Effects

# tree cover loss
mixed1 <- lmer(
  tree_cover_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation + wildfire + year + (1 | country),
  data = panel_data
)

summary(mixed1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: 
## tree_cover_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation +  
##     wildfire + year + (1 | country)
##    Data: panel_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 90399.1
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -14.0791  -0.0436  -0.0038   0.0361  21.3198 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance  Std.Dev.
##  country  (Intercept) 3.545e+10 188284  
##  Residual             3.156e+09  56182  
## Number of obs: 3624, groups:  country, 157
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                        Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)           5.373e+05  2.814e+05   1.910
## hard_commodities      7.360e+00  5.261e-01  13.989
## logging               1.273e+00  3.658e-02  34.813
## shifting_cultivation  1.136e+00  2.864e-02  39.676
## wildfire              9.960e-01  5.620e-03 177.205
## year                 -2.525e+02  1.397e+02  -1.807
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) hrd_cm loggng shftn_ wildfr
## hard_cmmdts  0.144                            
## logging      0.012 -0.420                     
## shftng_cltv  0.152 -0.137  0.050              
## wildfire     0.045 -0.156 -0.092  0.019       
## year        -0.999 -0.145 -0.015 -0.154 -0.045
## fit warnings:
## Some predictor variables are on very different scales: consider rescaling. 
## You may also use (g)lmerControl(autoscale = TRUE) to improve numerical stability.
#primary loss
mixed2 <- lmer(
  primary_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation + wildfire + year + (1 | country),
  data = panel_data
)

summary(mixed2)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: primary_loss_ha ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation +  
##     wildfire + year + (1 | country)
##    Data: panel_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 43965.9
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -13.9011  -0.0706  -0.0042   0.0654  12.3828 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance  Std.Dev.
##  country  (Intercept) 2.085e+10 144405  
##  Residual             4.046e+09  63606  
## Number of obs: 1748, groups:  country, 76
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                        Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)           1.018e+06  4.917e+05   2.071
## hard_commodities      5.037e+00  7.347e-01   6.855
## logging               4.977e-02  5.373e-02   0.926
## shifting_cultivation  3.842e-01  3.341e-02  11.499
## wildfire              4.528e-01  1.588e-02  28.515
## year                 -4.983e+02  2.443e+02  -2.040
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) hrd_cm loggng shftn_ wildfr
## hard_cmmdts  0.226                            
## logging     -0.082 -0.263                     
## shftng_cltv  0.206 -0.130  0.001              
## wildfire     0.048 -0.195 -0.044  0.004       
## year        -0.999 -0.227  0.079 -0.208 -0.048
## fit warnings:
## Some predictor variables are on very different scales: consider rescaling. 
## You may also use (g)lmerControl(autoscale = TRUE) to improve numerical stability.
#carbon emissions
mixed3 <- lmer(
  carbon_emissions_MgCO2e ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation + wildfire + year + (1 | country),
  data = panel_data
)

summary(mixed3)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: 
## carbon_emissions_MgCO2e ~ hard_commodities + logging + shifting_cultivation +  
##     wildfire + year + (1 | country)
##    Data: panel_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 139206.8
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -14.6361  -0.0465  -0.0066   0.0337  17.2423 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance  Std.Dev. 
##  country  (Intercept) 1.679e+16 129567107
##  Residual             2.323e+15  48201511
## Number of obs: 3624, groups:  country, 157
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                        Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)          -3.889e+08  2.412e+08  -1.612
## hard_commodities      6.387e+03  4.496e+02  14.207
## logging               1.226e+02  3.063e+01   4.004
## shifting_cultivation  7.448e+02  2.449e+01  30.417
## wildfire              3.266e+02  4.816e+00  67.809
## year                  2.049e+05  1.198e+05   1.710
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) hrd_cm loggng shftn_ wildfr
## hard_cmmdts  0.145                            
## logging      0.009 -0.420                     
## shftng_cltv  0.152 -0.137  0.050              
## wildfire     0.044 -0.157 -0.098  0.019       
## year        -0.999 -0.145 -0.013 -0.153 -0.044
## fit warnings:
## Some predictor variables are on very different scales: consider rescaling. 
## You may also use (g)lmerControl(autoscale = TRUE) to improve numerical stability.

Interaction Model

interaction_model <- lmer(
  carbon_emissions_MgCO2e ~ hard_commodities * gfw_aboveground_carbon_stocks_2000__Mg_C +
    year + (1 | country),
  data = panel_data
)
## Warning: Some predictor variables are on very different scales: consider rescaling. 
## You may also use (g)lmerControl(autoscale = TRUE) to improve numerical stability.
summary(interaction_model)
